narratives.name: Inflation & Geopolitical Hedge (Silver)

narratives.name: China AI Chip Export Controls
narratives.thesis: Ongoing US export controls on advanced AI chips to China create uncertainty and potential headwinds for semiconductor companies with significant exposure to the Chinese market.

narratives.name: Banks - Diversified Watchlist

Technology.thesis: The AI infrastructure buildout continues to be the dominant driver, with strong demand for chips, equipment, and related software. Quantum computing is emerging as a significant new investment area, while some software names show mixed signals.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
China export controls expand to advanced AI chips and equipment — would disrupt supply chains
Significant slowdown in enterprise software spending beyond current levels — indicates broader economic weakness

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: E-commerce platforms are showing resilience with AI-powered enhancements, while specialty retail faces headwinds. Auto manufacturers are navigating a complex environment, and residential construction is stable but sensitive to rates.
watchFor: Consumer spending data shows significant contraction (e.g., retail sales down >1% MoM)
Interest rates rise sharply, impacting housing and auto affordability
Layoffs accelerate, weakening consumer confidence

Communication Services.thesis: Digital content and advertising platforms are navigating regulatory scrutiny while expanding AI capabilities. Entertainment companies face geopolitical headwinds, but streaming continues to evolve.
watchFor: Major regulatory action against Big Tech platforms (e.g., antitrust fines)
Significant decline in digital advertising spending
Streaming subscriber growth stalls across the industry

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense continues to see strong demand due to geopolitical factors, while logistics companies are focusing on efficiency. Heavy machinery is mixed, benefiting from AI infrastructure but facing agricultural headwinds.
watchFor: Significant de-escalation of global geopolitical conflicts
Major downturn in global trade volumes
Sharp decline in infrastructure spending initiatives

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant growth for leading pharmaceutical companies, while healthcare plans show stability. Medical devices are facing some pressure.
watchFor: New, more effective GLP-1 competitors emerge unexpectedly
Regulatory pushback on GLP-1 drug pricing or access
Healthcare policy changes negatively impact managed care organizations

Financials.thesis: Credit services and brokerage firms are showing strength, while diversified banks navigate concerns around junk debt and rising rates. Regulatory changes are also a key watch item.
watchFor: Federal Reserve signals a more hawkish stance on interest rates
Significant increase in loan defaults or credit card delinquencies
Major regulatory fines or enforcement actions against large banks

Consumer Defensive.thesis: The consumer defensive sector is facing mixed signals, with some retailers backed by Wall Street while others contend with broader inflationary pressures and consumer sentiment.
watchFor: Core CPI print above 0.4% MoM for two consecutive months
Consumer confidence index drops below 90
Major retailers report unexpected inventory build-ups

Utilities.thesis: The utilities sector is seeing some activity around independent power producers, but no overarching bullish or bearish narrative is currently dominating flows.
watchFor: Major shift in energy policy towards renewables or fossil fuels
Significant changes in commodity prices (e.g., natural gas)
Interest rates move sharply, impacting utility financing costs

Basic Materials.thesis: Rare earth stocks are rallying on positive funding news and diminishing concerns about China's dominance, indicating a bullish sentiment for industrial metals and mining.
watchFor: China reasserts dominance in rare earth supply chain
Global demand for rare earths declines unexpectedly
New environmental regulations increase mining costs

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are currently influenced by a mix of inflation concerns and geopolitical developments, leading to a neutral to slightly positive outlook for silver, while gold lacks clear drivers.
watchFor: CPI print above 0.5% MoM for two consecutive months
US Dollar Index (DXY) breaks below 100
Major de-escalation of global geopolitical conflicts
